data.title: Western Midstream Partners declares $0.875 dividend
data.description:

  • Forward yield 8.89%
  • Payable Nov. 14; for shareholders of record Nov. 1; ex-div Nov. 1.

data.title: Dye & Durham surpasses $150M in annual recurring revenue
data.description:
• Dye & Durham Limited (TSX:DND:CA) said on Thursday it has surpassed $150 million in annual recurring revenue, a metric which includes revenues from subscriptions and minimum spend contracts.
  • The company continues to track towards an ARR of more than 50% of total revenue by the end of fiscal 2026.

data.title: Treasury yields march higher after latest retail sales report
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields climbed as investors sold off bonds following stronger-than-expected September retail sales data that bolstered confidence in the economy and the hopes that the Federal Reserve would be able to deliver a soft landing.

In early trading, the shorter-end more rate-sensitive U.S. 2-Year Treasury yield (US2Y) had pushed up 5 basis points to 4.00%, while the longer-end benchmark U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield (U10Y) had popped up by 5 basis points as well to 4.07%.

Both the US2Y and US10Y are back above 4.00%, and the US10Y also pushed back above its 100-day moving average.

Furthermore, see how other yields trade across the entire yield curve here.

The uptick in yields came as U.S. retail sales in the month of September grew by 0.4% from August to $714.4B, which topped the +0.3% consensus figure and accelerated from the +0.1% prior month’s level.

As Treasury yields rise, they exert downward pressure on bond prices, since the two instruments move inversely. As a result, outlined below are some notable Treasury and fixed-income-focused ETFs that investors and traders may wish to further monitor.

Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

Bond ETFs: (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), and (TIP).

data.title: Arista Networks continues to land data center deals: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) continues to gain traction in the cloud and data center networking space as Ethernet continues to take market share from InfiniBand, according to Morgan Stanley.

Morgan Stanley analysts reiterated its Overweight rating on Arista and bumped its price target on the networking solution company to $410 from $355.

"We believe that ANET valuation is not cheap at ~39x FY26 EPS (or 31x buyside FY26 EPS), with buy side estimates already high," said Morgan Stanley analysts Meta Marshall and Mary Lenox, in a Thursday investor note. "However, these data points are encouraging for multiple paths to growth and Arista's opportunity in Ethernet taking share from InfiniBand."

Arista has demonstrated steady growth this year, with shares more than doubling in value over the past 12 months. It closed at a record high of $416.93 on October 11. It closed 3.5% higher on Wednesday and was edging up again Thursday morning.

The networking company has also locked down several major cloud clients this year as artificial intelligence applications require larger and more complex data center configurations. It recently started trials on its fifth major cloud customer this year. It is working with Meta Platforms (META) on a 100,000 GPU cluster as well.

"We believe a next major catalyst would be ANET seeing 100K META cluster move to deployments/revenue," Marshall notes. "However, there continues to be uncertainty around the timing of deployments that is dependent on the bottlenecks cloud customers are facing of having the facilities/power/labor/expertise."

Arista is scheduled to release its third quarter 2024 financial results after markets close on November 7. For the quarter ended September 30, a consensus of analysts expects Arista to report adjusted earnings per share of $2.08 on revenue of $1.76B.

data.title: Travelers Companies stock climbs after strong Q3 earnings beat
data.description:
The Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV) stock accelerated 4.1% in Thursday premarket trading after the property and casualty insurer delivered Q3 earnings that jumped well above the average analyst estimate, owing to robust underwriting and investment income results.

Q3 core EPS of $5.24, blowing past the $3.59 consensus, more than doubled from $2.51 in Q2 and $1.95 a year before.

Revenue of $11.9B, also topping the $10.6B expected, rose from $11.3B in the prior quarter and $10.6B in the year-ago period.

"Our results benefited from a 10% increase in net earned premiums to a record $10.7 billion and an excellent combined ratio that improved nearly 8 points to 93.2%," said Chairman and CEO Alan Schnitzer. "Very strong underlying profitability and net favorable prior year development drove the improvement in our combined ratio."

Net earned premiums advanced to $10.7B from $10.2B in Q2 and $9.72B in Q3 of last year.

Net investment income was $904M, up from $885M in Q2 and $769M in Q3 2023.

Combined ratio improved to 93.2% from 100.2% in the previous three-month period and 101.0% a year earlier.

Catastrophe losses, which mainly resulted from Hurricane Helene and severe wind and hail storms in multiple states, eased to $939M on a pretax basis from $1.51B in Q2 and rose from $850M in Q3 2023.

Adjusted book value per share of $131.30 at Sept. 30, 2024, vs. $126.52 at June 30, 2024, and $115.78 at Sept. 30, 2023.

data.title: KeyCorp Q3 GAAP results hurt by portfolio repositioning, adjusted EPS beats
data.description:
KeyCorp's (NYSE:KEY) turned in a bottom-line loss due to losses on sales of securities in its portfolio repositioning in Q3 2024; however, its adjusted EPS topped the Wall Street consensus and increased on a Q/Q and Y/Y basis.

Q3 net interest income topped the average analyst estimate, while the portfolio repositioning cut into its noninterest income.

During the quarter, Key (KEY) received the initial $821M tranche of investment from Scotiabank (BNS).

Q3 adjusted EPS of $0.30, vs. the $0.22 consensus, increased from $0.25 in Q2 and $0.29 in last year's Q3. For the current quarter, losses stemming from its securities portfolio repositioning resulted in a GAAP loss of $0.47 per share.

Total revenue (taxable equivalent) of $695M, hurt by the loss on securities sales, sank from $1.53B in the previous quarter and $1.57B a year ago.

KeyCorp (KEY) now expects 2024 noninterest income to rise 6% or more, up from its prior guidance of up 5% or more. It still expects net interest income (taxable equivalent) to drop 2%-5% in 2024.

The bank now average deposits to rise 1%-2%, compared with its prior view of "stable. It still expects 2024 average loans to drop 7%-8% Y/Y.

Q3 net interest income (taxable equivalent) of $964M, beating the Visible Alpha estimate of $949M, increased from $899M in Q2 and $923M in Q3 2023.

Noninterest income plunged to -$269M, including a pretax loss of $918M on the sale of securities, from $627M in the prior quarter and $643M a year ago.

Trust and investment income of $140M rose 1% Q/Q and 89% Y/Y. Investment banking and debt placement fees of $171M climbed 36% Q/Q and 21% Y/Y. Cards and payments income of $84M slipped 1% Q/Q and 7% Y/Y.

KeyCorp's (KEY) provision for credit losses of $95M fell from $100M in the prior quarter and increased from $81M in the year-ago period.

Noninterest expense of $1.09B edged up from $1.08B in Q2 and dipped from $1.11B in last year's Q3.

Average deposits were $147.8B vs. $144.2B in Q2. its cost of total deposits increased to 2.39% from 2.28% in the previous quarter.

Average loans of $106.2B vs. $109.9B in Q2.

Earlier, KeyCorp Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.08, revenue of $695M

data.title: Curbline Properties initiated with Overweight rating at Piper on strong management
data.description:
Curbline Properties (NYSE:CURB), spun out of the retail REIT SITE Centers (SITC) recently, was initiated with an Overweight rating and $28 price target at Piper Sandler on the back of its strong management.

SITC had spun off its convenience store properties as CURB.

The investment bank and financial services company believes that "owning desirable convenience centers in high traffic submarkets will result in a significant re-rating of this CAPEX-lite retail model".

For the CURB management, Piper said it rarely sees a team in the REITs sector that leaves the security of an existing platform for a new entity with a novel concept.

SITC is likely to be liquidated in Piper's view.

"The beauty of CURB is high retention rates and lower leasing CAPEX associated with inline-only versus traditional shopping centers with large box anchors," Piper said in a research note.

"Given cap rate compression so far, the market seems to agree," said the investment bank.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analyst rating of Buy. Seeking Alpha authors as well as the Quant Rating system grades the stock as Hold.

CURB closed 0.77% lower on Wednesday at $23.15.

data.title: Sage Therapeutics to lay off one-third staff under reorganization
data.description:
Sage Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SAGE) announced a reorganization, including a workforce reduction of about 33%, to support the ongoing launch of ZURZUVAE for postpartum depression and to focus on pipeline development ahead of a clinical readout for dalzanemdor in Huntington’s Disease expected later this year.

The reorganization aims to strengthen the company's balance sheet, extend its cash runway, and position it for long-term growth.

The restructuring, set to be completed by Q4 2024, will impact over 165 employees (around 33% of the total workforce and 55% of R&D staff), including leadership changes.

Departing executives include Anne Marie Cook, Kimi Iguchi, Matt Lasmanis, Heinrich Schlieker, and Amy Schacterle. Chris Benecchi will become Chief Operating Officer, overseeing multiple departments, while Greg Shiferman and Vanessa Procter will assume expanded roles.

The stock is down 3.7% premarket.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: NFLX, EXPE and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Thursday advanced, as tech got a boost on strong quarterly results from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), the world's largest contract chipmaker. Here are some stocks to watch on Thursday:

  • Shares of Expedia (EXPE) climbed 6.5% in pre-market trading, after a report that ride-hailing giant Uber (UBER) had considered a possible takeover of the travel agency. According to a Financial Times report late Wednesday, Uber (UBER) has talked to advisers in recent months about a bid for Expedia (EXPE) after an acquisition of the travel agency was broached by a third party. As per the report, there has been no formal approach from Uber (UBER) and the ride-hailing firm's interest was described as being at a very early stage.
  • All eyes will be on Netflix (NFLX) stock, with the streaming leader scheduled to report Q3 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the Los Gatos, Calif.-based company to earn $5.13 per share on revenue of $9.77B. The latter metric is anticipated to rise 14.3% Y/Y. Wedbush estimates 5.5M net subscriber additions globally for Netflix (NFLX) in the quarter, versus a consensus of 4.5M. Investors will also be looking at the performance of the streamer's cheaper advertising tier, and any comments on it.
  • Shares of Blackstone (BX) added about 1% ahead of the opening bell. The world's largest alternative investment firm delivered Q3 distributable earnings per share that surpassed expectations. Blackstone's (BX) funds attracted inflows of $40.5B in the quarter, and the firm invested or committed $54B of capital - the highest in over two years. "The third quarter also represented the highest amount of overall fund appreciation in three years," top boss Stephen Schwarzman said. See how BX performed in charts.
  • Elevance Health (ELV) stock plunged nearly 13% ahead of market open. The health insurer stoked further concerns about high medical costs in the overall sector after slashing its full-year adjusted profit guidance. Managed care players have been dealing with increased costs associated with Medicaid plans as more patients seek medical procedures following the end of the pandemic-era policies. Chief executive Gail Boudreaux noted "unprecedented challenges" in the Medicaid business.

More on the markets

  • Are You Worried About An Overvalued Market?
  • Dow notches record close, S&P rises as small-cap rally, rotation helps Wall Street rebound

data.title: Iron ore sinks to three-week low as China demand sentiment wanes
data.description:
Iron ore futures fell below $100/ton in Asia on Thursday, as investors doubt whether China's latest stimulus moves will do enough to boost construction activity and steel demand.

China's housing minister announced a plan to expand a program to support the completion of unfinished housing projects to 4T yuan (~$560B), as the government tries to ease the country's real estate crisis.

However, analysts said the policies are focused on resolving the backlog of housing inventory, which provides little help for steel demand in the short term.

Iron ore futures (SCO:COM) in Singapore slumped 5% to $99.50/ton to the lowest since September 26, while copper, aluminum and zinc all dropped at least 1% on the London Metal Exchange.

Iron ore had rallied from a two-year low below $90/ton in late September to above $110, but prices have faded as a series of government briefings on economic policy fell short of expectations.

China's economy remains under pressure, with Q3 growth likely coming in at its weakest pace in six quarters, according to a Bloomberg survey.

Potentially relevant stocks include BHP (NYSE:BHP), Rio Tinto (RIO), Vale (VALE), Fortescue (OTCQX:FSUMF), Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) and Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY).
